title: Brent Anderson
content:
{{Top|rwp}}
'''Brent Eric Anderson''' (born [[June 15]], [[1950s#1955|1955]]) is a [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] illustrator who drew an issue cover in the [[Star Wars (1977)|''Marvel Star Wars'']] comic series, and he pencilled the first issue of [[Star Wars: Chewbacca (Dark Horse)|''Star Wars: Chewbacca'']] for [[Dark Horse Comics]].

His work in comics began in the 1970s on underground titles such as ''Venture''. In [[1979]] he began illustrating [[Wikipedia:Ka-Zar|''Ka-Zar the Savage'']] for Marvel and the graphic novel [[Wikipedia:God Loves, Man Kills|''God Loves, Man Kills'']] starring the [[Wikipedia:X-Men|''X-Men'']] as well as pencilling the monthly ''X-Men'' series for a time. He continued to work on various projects through the years, and in [[2005]] he co-created the acclaimed series [[Wikipedia:Astro City|''Astro City'']] initially published by [[Wikipedia:Image Comics|Image Comics]].
